{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Alekhine's Defence| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd| |rd|= |pd|pd|pd| |pd|pd|pd|pd|= | |nd| |pd| | | | |= | | | | |pl| | | |= | | |pl|pl| | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= |pl|pl| | | |pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= || }} = Alekhine's Defence = Now that the knight has left the d-file, the threat of 5...dxe5 6.dxe5 Qxd1+ 7.Kxd1, shattering White's pawns and preventing castling, must be dealt with. White has two main choices now: the aggressive Four Pawns Attack ([[/5. f4/]]) and the subtle Exchange Variation ([[/5. exd6/]]). ==Theory table== {{ChessTable}}. '''1.e4 Nf6 2.e5 Nd5 3.d4 d6 4. c4 Nb6'''
